source: Verified specifications from Fender spec sheets and blueprint archive. Scale 648mm, radius 241.3mm/304.8mm, bolt-on neck, 0 deg headstock confirmed 2026-03-29. Second Fender instrument in spec library after Telecaster.
notes: The Stratocaster (1954) introduced the contoured body with belly cut and forearm contour, setting it apart from the slab-body Telecaster. Features synchronized tremolo bridge (vibrato). The longer 648mm scale and rounder 241.3mm (9.5") fretboard radius are defining Fender characteristics. Three single-coil pickups in SSS configuration became the archetypal rock guitar sound.

vintage_62.description: Fender Stratocaster '62 Reissue — representative of the pre-CBS golden era (1954-1965). 7.25" vintage radius fretboard, 21 frets, smaller nut width. The 1962 Strat is considered peak vintage spec.

neck.depth_note: Neck-only depth. Add fretboard thickness (~6mm for Fender maple/rosewood board) for total stack.
neck.profile_note: 1962 necks transitioned from soft V at nut to C-shape by 12th fret.
neck.fretboard_radius_note: 7.25 inch radius is the vintage Fender standard — rounder than modern 9.5". Excellent for chord playing but tight for big bends.
neck.neck_joint_note: 4-bolt neck plate. Bolt-on allows neck removal for adjustment or replacement.
neck.neck_angle_note: Stratocaster uses a flat (0 deg) neck angle. Bridge height and shim set action.

headstock.angle_note: Fender headstock is flat (0 deg). String trees required on B and high-E.
headstock.string_tree_note: Single string tree on B and E strings. Modern versions may have two trees.

body.material_note: Sunburst finishes typically ash; solid colors typically alder.
body.contour_note: The Strat introduced both belly cut (back) and forearm contour (front), making it more comfortable than slab-body designs.

hardware.bridge_note: 6-saddle synchronized tremolo with spring cavity in back. Designed by Leo Fender for pitch vibrato.
hardware.pickup_selector_note: Vintage 3-way: neck, middle, bridge only. In-between positions discovered by players jamming the switch.

modern_standard.description: Fender American Standard / Professional — the modern benchmark Stratocaster. 22 frets, 9.5" or compound radius fretboard, modern C-profile neck. Represents current production standard.

neck.depth_note: Neck-only depth. Add fretboard thickness (~6mm) for total stack.
neck.fretboard_radius_note: 9.5 inch radius is the modern Fender standard — flatter than vintage 7.25". Better for bending, still comfortable for chords.
neck.compound_radius_note: Some American Professional models use compound 9.5"-14" radius.
neck.neck_joint_note: 4-bolt neck plate with micro-tilt adjustment on some models.

headstock.string_tree_note: Two string trees: one for B+E, one for D+G on modern specs.

hardware.bridge_note: Modern 6-saddle synchronized tremolo with bent steel saddles. Hardtail (through-body string) option available.
hardware.pickup_selector_note: 5-way selector: neck, neck+middle, middle, middle+bridge, bridge. Standard since 1977.

comparison_to_telecaster.body_contour: Strat has belly cut + forearm contour; Tele is slab body
comparison_to_telecaster.pickups: Strat SSS (3 single coils); Tele 2 single coils
comparison_to_telecaster.bridge: Strat synchronized tremolo; Tele 3-saddle (vintage) or 6-saddle hardtail
comparison_to_telecaster.fret_access: Strat double-cutaway better upper fret access; Tele single cutaway
comparison_to_telecaster.weight: Similar (~3.4kg), but Strat feels lighter due to contouring

comparison_to_gibson_les_paul.scale_length_note: Strat 648mm vs LP 628.65mm — Strat is brighter, higher tension
comparison_to_gibson_les_paul.fretboard_radius_comparison: Strat 241.3mm (9.5") vs LP 304.8mm (12") — Strat is rounder
comparison_to_gibson_les_paul.neck_joint: Strat bolt-on vs LP set-neck — different sustain and serviceability
comparison_to_gibson_les_paul.headstock_angle: Strat 0 deg (flat) vs LP 14-17 deg — Strat requires string trees
comparison_to_gibson_les_paul.body_construction: Strat solid slab vs LP carved maple cap on mahogany
comparison_to_gibson_les_paul.typical_weight: Strat ~3.4kg vs LP ~4.0-4.5kg — Strat significantly lighter
